IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: SNA EMPOWERS AND SUPPORTS SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS IN ADVANCING THE ACCESSIBILITY, QUALITY AND INTEGRITY OF SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO EMPOWER AND SUPPORT SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS IN ADVANCING THE ACCESSIBILITY, QUALITY AND INTEGRITY OF SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS.
IRS990.Desc: MEETINGS AND EDUCATION: PROVIDED TRAINING, EDUCATION AND NETWORKING OPPORTUNITIES FOR OUR MEMBERS THROUGHOUT THE YEAR WITH VIRTUAL TRAININGS AND IN-PERSON CONFERENCES. EACH CONFERENCE WAS FILLED WITH EDUCATIONAL SESSIONS THAT FOCUSED ON THE NEEDS OF OUR MEMBERSHIP BASE.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: COMMUNICATIONS: PRODUCES THE AWARD-WINNING SCHOOL NUTRITION MAGAZINE WHICH IS THE OFFICIAL PUBLICATION OF THE SCHOOL NUTRITION ASSOCIATION AND IS READ BY SNA'S 46,000 PLUS MEMBERS. THE MAGAZINE ALSO COUNTS AMONG ITS READERS SOME 2,000 NON-MEMBERS WITH AN INTEREST IN CHILD NUTRITION. READERS TURN TO SNA FOR THE LATEST INFORMATION ON A HOST OF ITEMS THAT AFFECT THE SUCCESSFUL OPERATION OF A SCHOOL FOOD SERVICE PROGRAM. ALSO, PRODUCE SEVERAL ENEWSLETTERS THAT UPDATE MEMBERS ON A DAILY OR WEEKLY BASIS WITH NEWS STORIES THAT MAY AFFECT THEIR CHILD NUTRITION PROGRAMS. THE NEWSLETTERS ARE OFFERED THROUGH AN EMAIL SUBSCRIPTION BASE SERVICE.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: GOVERNMENT AFFAIRS: SNA ADVOCATES FOR ISSUES AND LEGISLATION THAT DIRECTLY AFFECT SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS THROUGHOUT THE UNITED STATES. SNA REMAINS COMMITTED TO ADVOCATING FOR INCREASED FUNDING FOR CHILD NUTRITION PROGRAMS DUE TO THE INCREASE IN FOOD, MILK AND EQUIPMENT COSTS, COMBINED WITH HIGH LABOR AND BENEFITS COSTS. SNA HAS ENGAGED IN A DIALOGUE WITH SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS TO ADVOCATE FOR NATIONAL SCHOOL NUTRITION STANDARDS FOR SCHOOL MEALS AND OTHER FOODS AND BEVERAGES AVAILABLE IN SCHOOLS. SNA POSITIONS ITSELF AS A LEADER IN THE CHILD NUTRITION REAUTHORIZATION PROCESS BY OFFERING ADVOCACY TOOLS FOR OUR MEMBERS AS WELL AS STATING A STRONG POSITION TO CONGRESS ON THE IMPORTANCE OF CHILD NUTRITION PROGRAMS THAT WILL IMPROVE HEALTH OUTCOMES FOR CHILDREN. SNA ALSO SERVES AS THE GO TO SOURCE FOR OTHER MEDIUMS IN PROVIDING POSITIVE AND PROFESSIONAL IMAGES OF THE SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSION TO THE PUBLIC AT LARGE.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: MEMBERSHIP: SNA MEMBERSHIP PROVIDES EDUCATIONAL AND PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT RESOURCES, ADVOCACY AND NETWORKING TO HELP SUPPORT THE CAREER OF SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS AND SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS. SNA MEMBERS ARE PART OF SOMETHING BIGGER THAN JUST THEIR SCHOOL OR DISTRICT. THEY ARE JOINING THE 46,000+ COMMITTED MEMBERS FROM ACROSS THE COUNTRY WHO ARE FEEDING THE FUTURE.

MARKETING AND PUBLIC RELATIONS: SNA IS CONTINUOUSLY INVOLVED IN INVESTIGATING THE LATEST TRENDS FACTORS AND ISSUES IMPACTING SCHOOLNUTRITION. RESEARCH IS DESIGNED AND CONDUCTED TO MEET NEEDS OF ASSOCIATION MEMBERS AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC. SNA ALSO OFFERS A VARIETYOF MATERIALS TO ASSIST MEMBERS IN PROMOTING SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS AND HEALTHY SCHOOL MEALS. THE RESOURCES ASSISTS MEMBERS WITHIDENTIFYING NEW MARKETING OPPORTUNITIES, AND PROVIDE FRESH IDEAS ON WAYS TO EDUCATE THE COMMUNITY ABOUT SCHOOL NUTRITION. SNA ALSOCELEBRATES MEMBERS WHO WORK EVERY DAY TO ENSURE WELLNOURISHED STUDENTS ACROSS THE COUNTRY ARE PREPARED FOR SUCCESS THROUGH THEAWARDS PROGRAM. INDIVIDUALS MAY BE NOMINATED BY PEERS STAFF, COLLEAGUES, OR SUPERVISORS FOR THESE AWARDS.

CERTIFICATE AND CREDENTIALING: SNA DEVELOPS AND ENCOURAGES THE HIGHEST STANDARDS IN SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS AND PROVIDESEDUCATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES TO ENSURE THE PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT OF ITS MEMBERS. SNA'S CERTIFICATE PROGRAM IS BASED ON THE STANDARDSOF PRACTICE AND INDICATORS IN THE KEYS TO EXCELLENCE IN SCHOOL FOOD AND NUTRITION PROGRAMS THAT DEFINE NATIONALLY ACCEPTEDSTANDARDS FOR QUALITY PROGRAMS. SNA ESTABLISHED THE SCHOOL NUTRITION SPECIALIST (SNS) CREDENTIALING PROGRAM TO ENHANCE THEPROFESSIONAL IMAGE OF SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS, ELEVATE PROFESSIONAL STANDARDS AND ENHANCE INDIVIDUAL PERFORMANCE. THE SNSCREDENTIAL IS A MARK OF EXCELLENCE AND ACHIEVEMENT THAT REFLECTS WHAT IT TAKES TO MANAGE SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS IN TODAY'SCHALLENGING CLIMATE. THE SNS CREDENTIALING EXAM EVALUATES CANDIDATES' KNOWLEDGE AND SKILLS REQUIRED TO PERFORM SPECIFIC JOB ACTIVITIESRELATED TO MANAGING OR DIRECTING SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS.

BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT: SNA'S INDUSTRY PARTNERS ARE VITAL TO FURTHERING THE MISSION OF THE ASSOCIATION. THE INDUSTRY RELATIONS AREA ISRESPONSIBLE FOR WORKING AND SERVICING INDUSTRY PARTNERS WITH INDUSTRY MEMBERSHIPS, MEETING SPONSORSHIPS, PARTNERSHIP PROPOSALS ANDTHE PATRON PROGRAM.

Form990PartVIISectionAGrp.TitleTxt: VP, GOV. AFFAIRS AND MEDIA RELATIONS
CHIEF OF STAFF / VP, STRATEGY & GOV.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE SCHOOL NUTRITION ASSOCIATION PAC IS A SEPARATELY SEGREGATED FUND AS DESCRIBED IN SECTION 527(F)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. THE PAC SUPPORTS THE ELECTION CAMPAIGNS OF CANDIDATES WHO SUPPORT SNA'S MISSION. SNA PROVIDES OFFICE SPACE, USE OF EQUIPMENT AND PERSONNEL TO THE PAC AT NO CHARGE.

IN 1986 AN ENDOWMENT WAS ESTABLISHED TO HONOR US REPRESENTATIVE CARL D PERKINS, A STRONG SUPPORTER OF CHILD NUTRITION PROGRAMS. FUNDS ARE USED TO SUPPORT ADVOCACY AND PUBLIC RELATION EFFORTS REGARDING THE IMPORTANCE OF SCHOOL NUTRITION PROGRAMS.

THE ASSOCIATION IS EXEMPT FROM THE PAYMENT OF INCOME TAXES ON ITS EXEMPT ACTIVITIES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(4) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. HOWEVER, THE ASSOCIATION IS SUBJECT TO TAXES ON ITS UNRELATED BUSINESS ACTIVITIES WHICH CONSIST PRINCIPALLY OF ADVERTISING. THE ASSOCIATIONS' INCOME TAX RETURN IS SUBJECT TO REVIEW AND EXAMINATION BY FEDERAL AND STATE AUTHORITIES. THE ASSOCIATION IS NOT AWARE OF ANY ACTIVITIES THAT WOULD JEOPARDIZE ITS TAX-EXEMPT STATUS.
BOARD MEETING MINUTES AS WELL AS AN ANNUAL BUDGETING PROCESS.

RltdOrgOfficerTrstKeyEmplGrp.TitleTxt: VP, GOV. AFFAIRS AND MEDIA RELATIONS
CHIEF OF STAFF / VP, STRATEGY & GOV.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ASSOCIATION HAS AN INTERNAL REVENUE CODE SECTION 457 DEFERRED COMPENSATION PLAN UNDER WHICH THE CURRENT CHIEF EXECUTIVE OFFICER (CEO) IS A PARTICIPANT. PLAN ASSETS ARE THE PROPERTY OF THE ASSOCIATION AND SUBJECT TO THE CLAIMS OF THOSE GENERAL CREDITORS OF THE ASSOCIATION. THE DEFERRED COMPENSATION IS NOT AVAILABLE TO PARTICIPANTS UNTIL RETIREMENT, SEPARATION FROM SERVICE, DEATH, DISABILITY, OR UNFORESEEABLE EMERGENCY. FUNDS OF THE PLANS ARE INVESTED IN SEPARATE MUTUAL FUND ACCOUNTS FOR EACH PARTICIPANT AND ARE RECORDED AT FAIR VALUE, TOGETHER WITH THE RELATED LIABILITY, IN THE ACCOMPANYING STATEMENTS OF FINANCIAL POSITION. DURING THE CALENDAR YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2023, CONTRIBUTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,500 WERE MADE TO THE PLAN.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 1A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE CONSISTS OF THE PRESIDENT-WHO SERVES AS CHAIR,PRESIDENT-ELECT, VICE PRESIDENT, SECRETARY TREASURER AND TWO DIRECTORS IN THE SECOND OR THIRD YEAR OF THEIR TERMS, ELECTED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS. THE CEO SERVES AS A NONVOTING MEMBER. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE SHALL ASSIST THE BOARD IN MANAGING SNA ON ISSUES THAT ARE TIME SENSITIVE BETWEEN OFFICIAL BOARD MEETINGS AND, IN SUCH CASES, SHALL HAVE THE AUTHORITY OF THE BOARD PROVIDED THAT THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE DOES NOT ALTER OR ACT CONTRARY TO ESTABLISHED BOARD POLICIES. THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE SHALL ALSO CARRY OUT TASKS REFERRED TO IT BY THE BOARD. ACTIONS OF THE EXECUTIVE COMMITTEE SHALL BE COMMUNICATED TO THE BOARD AT THE NEXT BOARD MEETING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION HAS MEMBERS THAT HAVE THE RIGHTS TO ELECT THE GOVERNING BODY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION HAS MEMBERS WHO ARE ENTITLED TO VOTE FOR THE ELECTION OF ITS DIRECTORS AND OFFICERS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DELEGATES CHOSEN BY STATE AFFILIATES DETERMINE REPRESENTATION IN THE ANNUAL DELEGATE ASSEMBLY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A DRAFT OF FORM 990 WILL BE SHARED AND REVIEWED BY THE FULL BOARD OF DIRECTORS PRIOR TO FILING WITH IRS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CONFLICT OF INTEREST STATEMENTS ARE SIGNED ANNUALLY BY BOARD OF DIRECTORS. STANDARDS OF BUSINESS CONDUCT IS SENT TO POTENTIAL BOARD MEMBERS PRIOR TO SUBMITTING NAME TO THE LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT COMMITTEE. BOARD OF DIRECTORS AND THE CEO MONITOR THE ADHERENCE TO THE POLICY. CONFLICT ISSUES ARE BROUGHT FORTH TO THE ETHICS COMMITTEE AND THE PROCESS INCLUDES REVIEW OF SITUATION AND ADVISEMENT OF REQUIRED ACTION SO THAT CONFLICT NO LONGER EXISTS. THIS COULD INCLUDE A MEMBER BEING REMOVED FROM THE DISCUSSION OR RESIGNING FROM THE BOARD.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FOR THE CEO'S COMPENSATION AND ALL STAFF, THE ORGANIZATION USES AN INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION CONSULTANT TO CONDUCT A SALARY REVIEW OF MARKET DATA FOR NON-PROFIT ASSOCIATIONS OF LIKE SIZE LOCATED IN THE WASHINGTON, DC AREA. THIS PROCESS RECENTLY TOOK PLACE IN 2023. FOR ALL STAFF, AN INDEPENDENT COMPENSATION CONSULTANT CONDUCTED A MARKET SALARY REVIEW. THE CONSULTING GROUP PROVIDED GUIDANCE AS TO HOW TO ROLL DATA INTO FUTURE PERIODS TO ENSURE SNA'S SALARIES ARE WITHIN MARKET. DOLLARS ARE INCLUDED IN THE ANNUAL OPERATING BUDGET APPROVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS TO ALLOW FOR ADJUSTMENTS. SNA'S CEO REVIEWED THE MARKET SALARY DATA AND APPROVES MARKET ADJUSTMENTS BASED ON DOLLARS BUDGETED AND AVAILABLE TO SUPPORT SUCH ADJUSTMENTS. THIS PROCESS TOOK PLACE IN 2023 FOR ALL STAFF AND AS STAFF POSITION ARE VACATED THEY ARE REASSESS BY CONSULTANT TO CONFIRM CURRENT MARKET SALARY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY POSTED ON ORGANIZATION'S WEBSITE. THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON WRITTEN REQUEST.
CONTRACT SERVICES EXPENSE: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 1,018,769. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 318,834. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 1,337,603.

IdRelatedTaxExemptOrgGrp.PrimaryActivitiesTxt: PROVIDE RESOURCES TO SCHOOL NUTRITION PROFESSIONALS
